project.name: Apache Kafka
project.description: Open-source distributed event streaming platform for high-performance data pipelines, streaming analytics, data integration, and mission-critical applications

nodes.name: build.gradle
nodes.summary: Root Gradle build script defining plugins, dependencies, compilation settings, and test configurations for all Kafka subprojects.

nodes.name: settings.gradle
nodes.summary: Gradle settings file listing all subprojects and configuring Develocity build scans for the Kafka project.

nodes.name: gradle.properties
nodes.summary: Defines project group, version (4.4.0-SNAPSHOT), Scala version, and JVM args for Gradle builds.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Project README describing Apache Kafka as a distributed event streaming platform with build and quickstart instructions.

nodes.name: dependencies.gradle
nodes.summary: Centralized dependency version management defining all third-party library versions used across Kafka subprojects.

nodes.name: broker.properties
nodes.summary: Configuration template for a Kafka broker node running in combined (broker+controller) or broker-only mode.

nodes.name: connect-console-sink.properties
nodes.summary: Kafka Connect configuration for a console sink connector that writes messages to stdout.

nodes.name: connect-console-source.properties
nodes.summary: Kafka Connect configuration for a console source connector that reads from stdin.

nodes.name: connect-distributed.properties
nodes.summary: Configuration for running Kafka Connect in distributed mode with group coordination and offset storage topics.

nodes.name: connect-file-sink.properties
nodes.summary: Kafka Connect configuration for a file sink connector writing topic data to a local file.

nodes.name: connect-file-source.properties
nodes.summary: Kafka Connect configuration for a file source connector reading data from a local file.

nodes.name: connect-log4j2.yaml
nodes.summary: Log4j2 logging configuration for Kafka Connect workers.

nodes.name: connect-mirror-maker.properties
nodes.summary: Configuration for MirrorMaker 2 dedicated cluster replication between source and target Kafka clusters.

nodes.name: connect-standalone.properties
nodes.summary: Configuration for running Kafka Connect in standalone (single-process) mode.

nodes.name: consumer.properties
nodes.summary: Sample configuration for a Kafka consumer client with group and connection settings.

nodes.name: controller.properties
nodes.summary: Configuration template for a KRaft controller-only node managing cluster metadata.

nodes.name: log4j2.yaml
nodes.summary: Main Log4j2 logging configuration for Kafka broker with rolling file appenders and per-package log levels.

nodes.name: producer.properties
nodes.summary: Sample configuration for a Kafka producer client with serialization and connection settings.

nodes.name: server.properties
nodes.summary: Legacy server configuration template for Kafka broker (combined mode) with KRaft settings.

nodes.name: tools-log4j2.yaml
nodes.summary: Log4j2 logging configuration for Kafka CLI tools with WARN-level console output.

nodes.name: common.py
nodes.summary: Shared utility functions for Docker image build scripts including command execution and temp directory management.

nodes.name: docker_build_test.py
nodes.summary: Script to build and test Kafka Docker images from a given Kafka binary archive URL.

nodes.name: docker_official_image_build_test.py
nodes.summary: Script to build and test Docker Official Images for Kafka using the official image format.

nodes.name: docker_release.py
nodes.summary: Script to promote and push Kafka Docker images to registries for release.

nodes.name: extract_docker_official_image_artifact.py
nodes.summary: Extracts artifacts needed for building Docker Official Images from Kafka releases.

nodes.name: generate_kafka_pr_template.py
nodes.summary: Generates PR templates for Docker Official Image updates in the docker-library/official-images repo.

nodes.name: prepare_docker_official_image_source.py
nodes.summary: Prepares source files and Dockerfiles for Docker Official Image submissions.

nodes.name: docker/README.md
nodes.summary: Documentation for Kafka Docker image build process, usage, and release workflow.

nodes.name: kafka-server-start.sh
nodes.summary: Shell script to start a Kafka server/broker process using kafka-run-class.sh with a given properties file.

nodes.name: kafka-run-class.sh
nodes.summary: Core launcher script that sets up classpath, JVM options, and executes any Kafka Java class.

nodes.name: kafka-topics.sh
nodes.summary: Shell script to manage Kafka topics (create, list, describe, delete) via kafka-run-class.sh.

nodes.name: kafka-console-producer.sh
nodes.summary: Shell script to start a console producer that reads from stdin and publishes to a Kafka topic.

nodes.name: kafka-console-consumer.sh
nodes.summary: Shell script to start a console consumer that reads from a Kafka topic and writes to stdout.

nodes.name: kafka-configs.sh
nodes.summary: Shell script to manage Kafka dynamic configuration overrides for brokers, topics, and clients.

nodes.name: build.yml
nodes.summary: Reusable CI workflow that compiles, checks, and runs tests for Kafka across multiple JDK versions.

nodes.name: ci-complete.yml
nodes.summary: CI completion gate workflow that aggregates results from all CI jobs to determine overall pass/fail status.

nodes.name: ci.yml
nodes.summary: Main CI entrypoint workflow triggered on push/PR to trunk that dispatches the build workflow.

nodes.name: deflake.yml
nodes.summary: Workflow to repeatedly run flaky tests for debugging and deflaking purposes.

nodes.name: docker_build_and_test.yml
nodes.summary: CI workflow to build and test Kafka Docker images on demand via workflow_dispatch.

nodes.name: docker_official_image_build_and_test.yml
nodes.summary: CI workflow to build and test Docker Official Images for Kafka.

nodes.name: docker_promote.yml
nodes.summary: Workflow to promote Docker images to production registries after successful testing.

nodes.name: TopicMetadataRequestManager.java
nodes.summary: Manages topic metadata requests for consumer listTopics and partitionsFor API calls with retry and timeout handling.

nodes.name: TopicMetadataRequestManager
nodes.summary: Implements RequestManager to handle topic metadata requests with backoff and expiration.

nodes.name: TopicPartitionReplica.java
nodes.summary: Immutable value class representing a topic partition replica identified by topic, partition, and broker ID.

nodes.name: TopicPartitionReplica
nodes.summary: Value object for topic name, partition number, and broker ID of a replica.

nodes.name: AdminClientConfig.java
nodes.summary: Configuration class for Kafka AdminClient defining all admin client config properties and defaults.

nodes.name: AdminClientConfig
nodes.summary: Extends AbstractConfig to define configuration properties for the Kafka admin client.

nodes.name: AdminApiHandler.java
nodes.summary: Interface defining the handler contract for admin API operations including request building and response handling.

nodes.name: AdminApiHandler
nodes.summary: Interface for building admin API requests and handling their responses with key-based lookup.

nodes.name: AdminFetchMetricsManager.java
nodes.summary: Simple metrics manager for tracking admin client fetch request latencies.

nodes.name: AdminFetchMetricsManager
nodes.summary: Records per-node latency metrics for admin fetch operations.

nodes.name: AdminMetadataManager.java
nodes.summary: Manages cluster metadata for the admin client, handling metadata updates and node selection.

nodes.name: AdminMetadataManager
nodes.summary: Tracks cluster metadata state for the admin client and determines when metadata refresh is needed.

nodes.name: ConsumerHeartbeatRequestManager.java
nodes.summary: Manages consumer group heartbeat requests for the new consumer group protocol.

nodes.name: ConsumerHeartbeatRequestManager
nodes.summary: Builds and sends ConsumerGroupHeartbeat requests to maintain group membership.

nodes.name: CoordinatorRequestManager.java
nodes.summary: Manages FindCoordinator requests to discover the group coordinator node for consumers.

nodes.name: CoordinatorRequestManager
nodes.summary: Discovers and tracks the group coordinator node, issuing FindCoordinator requests as needed.

nodes.name: FetchRequestManager.java
nodes.summary: Manages fetch requests for consumer data retrieval, coordinating with fetch sessions.

nodes.name: FetchRequestManager
nodes.summary: Implements RequestManager to create and send fetch requests for consuming messages.

nodes.name: StreamsGroupHeartbeatRequestManager.java
nodes.summary: Manages heartbeat requests for the Kafka Streams group protocol including topology and task assignment.

nodes.name: StreamsGroupHeartbeatRequestManager
nodes.summary: Builds and sends StreamsGroupHeartbeat requests with topology info and task assignments.

nodes.name: TopicMetadataFetcher.java
nodes.summary: Legacy synchronous topic metadata fetcher used by the old consumer implementation.

nodes.name: TopicMetadataFetcher
nodes.summary: Fetches topic metadata synchronously using ConsumerNetworkClient for the legacy consumer.

nodes.name: AlterReplicaLogDirsRequest.java
nodes.summary: Request class for the AlterReplicaLogDirs API to move partition replicas between log directories.

nodes.name: AlterReplicaLogDirsRequest
nodes.summary: Encapsulates an AlterReplicaLogDirs API request with partition-to-directory mappings.

nodes.name: AlterReplicaLogDirsResponse.java
nodes.summary: Response class for the AlterReplicaLogDirs API containing per-partition error results.

nodes.name: AlterReplicaLogDirsResponse
nodes.summary: Encapsulates the AlterReplicaLogDirs API response with per-partition results.

nodes.name: DescribeTopicPartitionsRequest.java
nodes.summary: Request class for the DescribeTopicPartitions API to get detailed partition information.

nodes.name: DescribeTopicPartitionsRequest
nodes.summary: Encapsulates a DescribeTopicPartitions API request with topic list and cursor support.

nodes.name: DescribeTopicPartitionsResponse.java
nodes.summary: Response class for the DescribeTopicPartitions API containing partition details and cursors.

nodes.name: DescribeTopicPartitionsResponse
nodes.summary: Encapsulates DescribeTopicPartitions API response with partition info and pagination cursor.

nodes.name: ClientCredentialsRequestFormatterFactory.java
nodes.summary: Factory that creates OAuth client credentials request formatters based on configuration.

nodes.name: ClientCredentialsRequestFormatterFactory
nodes.summary: Creates appropriate OAuth token request formatters based on grant type configuration.

nodes.name: CommonNameLoggingTrustManagerFactoryWrapper.java
nodes.summary: TrustManagerFactory wrapper that logs SSL certificate common names for debugging connections.

nodes.name: CommonNameLoggingTrustManagerFactoryWrapper
nodes.summary: Wraps TrustManagerFactory to log certificate common names and fingerprints during SSL handshakes.

nodes.name: ClientRequest.java
nodes.summary: Represents a client-level request being sent to a Kafka broker with metadata like destination and timing.

nodes.name: ClientRequest
nodes.summary: Immutable value class holding a network request with destination, creation time, and callback info.

nodes.name: ClientResponse.java
nodes.summary: Represents a response from a Kafka broker including the response body and request correlation metadata.

nodes.name: ClientResponse
nodes.summary: Holds a broker response with request header, response body, timing info, and disconnect status.

nodes.name: CommonClientConfigs.java
nodes.summary: Defines configuration constants and documentation shared across all Kafka client types.

nodes.name: CommonClientConfigs
nodes.summary: Contains common configuration keys, defaults, and documentation for producer, consumer, and admin clients.

nodes.name: DescribeTopicPartitionsRequestHandler.java
nodes.summary: Server-side handler for DescribeTopicPartitions API requests.

nodes.name: DescribeTopicPartitionsRequestHandler
nodes.summary: Handles DescribeTopicPartitions requests on the broker, returning partition metadata.

nodes.name: ConfigAdminManager.scala
nodes.summary: Manages dynamic configuration changes for brokers, topics, and other entities via admin APIs.

nodes.name: ConfigAdminManager
nodes.summary: Handles IncrementalAlterConfigs and DescribeConfigs requests for broker/topic configuration.

nodes.name: ReplicaAlterLogDirsManager.scala
nodes.summary: Manages fetcher threads for moving replicas between log directories on the same broker.

nodes.name: ReplicaAlterLogDirsManager
nodes.summary: Creates and manages fetcher threads for intra-broker log directory reassignment.

nodes.name: ReplicaFetcherManager.scala
nodes.summary: Manages replica fetcher threads for replicating data from leader brokers.

nodes.name: ReplicaFetcherManager
nodes.summary: Creates and manages fetcher threads that replicate partitions from leader brokers.

nodes.name: TestRaftRequestHandler.scala
nodes.summary: Test request handler for the Raft-based test server used in integration testing.

nodes.name: TestRaftRequestHandler
nodes.summary: Handles API requests for the TestRaftServer used in testing scenarios.

nodes.name: ClientRequestQuotaManager.java
nodes.summary: Manages client request rate quotas, tracking request processing time and throttling clients that exceed their quota allocation.

nodes.name: ClientRequestQuotaManager
nodes.summary: Extends ClientQuotaManager to enforce per-user/client-id request time quotas, converting nanoseconds to percentage utilization.

nodes.name: ClientQuotaMetadataManager.scala
nodes.summary: Processes client quota metadata records from the metadata log and updates quota managers and connection quotas accordingly.

nodes.name: ClientQuotaMetadataManager
nodes.summary: Consumes ClientQuotasDelta from the metadata log, dispatching IP and user/client-id quota updates to the appropriate quota managers.

nodes.name: LogManagerBuilder.java
nodes.summary: Builder pattern for constructing LogManager instances with configurable log directories, retention, flush, and recovery settings.

nodes.name: LogManagerBuilder
nodes.summary: Fluent builder that validates required parameters and constructs a LogManager with log directories, schedulers, and storage configurations.

nodes.name: ReplicaManagerBuilder.java
nodes.summary: Builder pattern for constructing ReplicaManager instances with required dependencies like log manager, metadata cache, and partition management.

nodes.name: ReplicaManagerBuilder
nodes.summary: Fluent builder for ReplicaManager, primarily used in tests to construct instances with minimal configuration.

nodes.name: LogManager.scala
nodes.summary: Companion object factory that creates a Java LogManager from KafkaConfig, extracting and validating log configuration parameters.

nodes.name: LogManager
nodes.summary: Scala companion object that constructs the Java LogManager by extracting configuration from KafkaConfig including cleaner, transaction, and producer state settings.

nodes.name: KafkaRaftManager.scala
nodes.summary: Manages the KRaft consensus protocol lifecycle including Raft log, network channel, and client initialization for metadata replication.

nodes.name: KafkaRaftManager
nodes.summary: Implements RaftManager by wiring together the KafkaRaftClient, network channel, raft log, and expiration service for KRaft consensus.

nodes.name: AbstractFetcherManager.scala
nodes.summary: Manages a pool of fetcher threads for replicating data from leader brokers, handling partition assignment and thread lifecycle.

nodes.name: AbstractFetcherManager
nodes.summary: Abstract base managing a map of fetcher threads keyed by broker-id and fetcher-id, supporting dynamic resizing, partition migration, and lag tracking.

nodes.name: AutoTopicCreationManager.scala
nodes.summary: Handles automatic creation of internal and user topics by sending CreateTopics requests to the controller with proper configuration.

nodes.name: DefaultAutoTopicCreationManager
nodes.summary: Default implementation that creates topics asynchronously via controller requests, with inflight deduplication and error caching for streams internal topics.

nodes.name: BrokerServer.scala
nodes.summary: Main broker server class for KRaft mode that orchestrates startup/shutdown of all broker components including replica manager, coordinators, and network.

nodes.name: BrokerServer
nodes.summary: KRaft-mode broker that initializes and manages all broker subsystems: log management, replication, group/transaction/share coordination, quotas, and network processing.

nodes.name: ConfigHandler.scala
nodes.summary: Defines config change handlers for topics, brokers, client metrics, and groups that process dynamic configuration updates.

nodes.name: TopicConfigHandler
nodes.summary: Processes topic configuration changes, updating log configs and managing remote log manager state transitions when remote storage settings change.

nodes.name: ControllerApis.scala
nodes.summary: Handles all API requests on the controller listener, implementing controller-side logic for topic, config, ACL, and quota management operations.

nodes.name: ControllerApis
nodes.summary: Request handler for the controller listener that delegates API requests to the QuorumController for topics, configs, ACLs, and cluster operations.

nodes.name: ControllerConfigurationValidator.scala
nodes.summary: Validates dynamic configuration changes for topics, brokers, client metrics, and groups on the controller before persisting to the metadata log.

nodes.name: ControllerConfigurationValidator
nodes.summary: Implements ConfigurationValidator to validate topic, broker, client metrics, and group config changes using LogConfig and GroupConfig validation.

nodes.name: ControllerServer.scala
nodes.summary: KRaft controller server that manages the QuorumController, metadata publishing, authorization, and controller network endpoints.

nodes.name: ControllerServer
nodes.summary: KRaft-mode controller that initializes the QuorumController, sets up metadata publishers, and manages controller lifecycle including socket server and authorizer.

nodes.name: DynamicBrokerConfig.scala
nodes.summary: Manages dynamic broker configuration that can be changed at runtime without restart, supporting per-broker and cluster-default overrides.

nodes.name: DynamicBrokerConfig
nodes.summary: Handles runtime broker config updates by maintaining current configs, validating changes, and notifying registered reconfigurable components.

nodes.name: KafkaRaftServer.scala
nodes.summary: Top-level KRaft server that bootstraps metadata, initializes log directories, and manages controller/broker server lifecycle based on process.roles.

nodes.name: KafkaRaftServer
nodes.summary: Entry point for KRaft mode that constructs SharedServer, ControllerServer, and/or BrokerServer based on configured process roles.

nodes.name: ClientQuotaManagerConfig.java
nodes.summary: Record class holding client quota manager configuration: number of quota samples and window size in seconds.

nodes.name: ClientQuotaManagerConfig
nodes.summary: Java record encapsulating quota sampling configuration with defaults from QuotaConfig.

nodes.name: DynamicProducerStateManagerConfig.java
nodes.summary: BrokerReconfigurable that dynamically updates producer ID expiration and transaction verification settings at runtime.

nodes.name: DynamicProducerStateManagerConfig
nodes.summary: Implements BrokerReconfigurable to dynamically update producer ID expiration and transaction partition verification settings.

nodes.name: ReplicationQuotaManagerConfig.java
nodes.summary: Configuration class for replication quota management with bytes-per-second defaults and sampling parameters.

nodes.name: ReplicationQuotaManagerConfig
nodes.summary: Holds replication quota settings: default bytes per second, number of samples, and window size.

nodes.name: NetworkPartitionMetadataClient.java
nodes.summary: Network-based PartitionMetadataClient that fetches partition offsets from leader brokers using ListOffsets requests with retry and backoff.

nodes.name: NetworkPartitionMetadataClient
nodes.summary: Implements PartitionMetadataClient using a KafkaClient to send ListOffsets requests to partition leaders with exponential backoff retry.

nodes.name: SocketServerConfigs.java
nodes.summary: Defines socket server configuration constants and ConfigDef entries for listeners, ports, buffer sizes, and connection limits.

nodes.name: SocketServerConfigs
nodes.summary: Static configuration class defining listener, port, buffer, and connection configuration properties for the Kafka socket server.

nodes.name: ApiVersionManager.java
nodes.summary: Interface defining the contract for managing supported API versions, feature sets, and generating ApiVersionsResponse for clients.

nodes.name: ApiVersionManager
nodes.summary: Interface for determining which API versions are enabled based on listener type and providing ApiVersionsResponse to clients.

nodes.name: BrokerLifecycleManager.java
nodes.summary: Manages broker state machine (registration, heartbeats, fencing, controlled shutdown) communicating with the active controller.

nodes.name: BrokerLifecycleManager
nodes.summary: Owns broker state transitions, sending registration and heartbeat requests to the controller and managing fencing, unfencing, and controlled shutdown.

nodes.name: ClientMetricsManager.java
nodes.summary: Manages client telemetry subscriptions and metrics collection, handling GetTelemetrySubscriptions and PushTelemetry requests from clients.

nodes.name: ClientMetricsManager
nodes.summary: Processes client telemetry subscription configurations and handles telemetry push/pull requests, exporting metrics via configured exporters.

nodes.name: DefaultApiVersionManager.java
nodes.summary: Default ApiVersionManager implementation for brokers that combines controller API versions with broker features and metadata cache.

nodes.name: DefaultApiVersionManager
nodes.summary: Broker ApiVersionManager that constructs ApiVersionsResponse by merging controller API versions, broker features, and finalized features from metadata cache.

nodes.name: FetchManager.java
nodes.summary: Manages fetch sessions for consumer fetch requests, supporting full, incremental, and sessionless fetch contexts with a sharded cache.

nodes.name: FetchManager
nodes.summary: Creates appropriate FetchContext (full, incremental, or sessionless) based on request metadata, managing a FetchSessionCache for incremental fetches.

nodes.name: KRaftTopicCreator.java
nodes.summary: KRaft implementation of topic creation that forwards CreateTopics requests to the controller, with envelope wrapping for authorization context preservation.

nodes.name: KRaftTopicCreator
nodes.summary: Forwards CreateTopics requests to the KRaft controller, wrapping in envelopes when principal context needs preservation.

nodes.name: LogDeleteRecordsResult.java
nodes.summary: Simple data class representing the result of a log delete records operation.

nodes.name: NodeToControllerChannelManagerImpl.java
nodes.summary: Implementation of NodeToControllerChannelManager that manages a network client connection from broker nodes to the active controller.

nodes.name: NodeToControllerChannelManagerImpl
nodes.summary: Concrete implementation managing network channel from broker to controller, handling connection setup, request sending, and controller discovery.

nodes.name: NodeToControllerRequestThread.java
nodes.summary: Background thread that processes queued requests from broker to controller, handling retries and response callbacks.

nodes.name: PartitionFetchState.java
nodes.summary: Record class tracking partition fetch offset and state (truncating, delayed, or ready for fetch) used by replica fetcher threads.

nodes.name: ControllerRequestCompletionHandler.java
nodes.summary: Interface defining callback methods for handling completion of requests sent to the controller.

nodes.name: ServerLogConfigs.java
nodes.summary: Constants and documentation for broker-side log configuration properties like log.dirs, num.partitions, retention settings.

nodes.name: ServerTopicConfigSynonyms.java
nodes.summary: Mapping between broker-level log configs and their topic-level config synonyms.

nodes.name: PartitionMetadataClient.java
nodes.summary: Interface for fetching partition metadata including leader and replica information.

nodes.name: ClientIdAndBroker.java
nodes.summary: Simple data class combining a client ID with a broker identifier for metrics tracking.

nodes.name: EligibleLeaderReplicasVersion.java
nodes.summary: Feature version enum for Eligible Leader Replicas (ELR) feature (KIP-966), defining version levels and metadata version dependencies.

nodes.name: NodeToControllerChannelManager.java
nodes.summary: Interface defining the contract for managing communication channels from broker nodes to the active controller.

nodes.name: NodeToControllerChannelManager
nodes.summary: Interface for sending requests from brokers to controller with start/shutdown lifecycle and API version discovery.

nodes.name: TopicIdPartition.java
nodes.summary: Record combining a topic UUID with a partition index for unique partition identification.

nodes.name: DelegationTokenManagerConfigs.java
nodes.summary: Configuration constants for delegation token management including token lifetime and secret key settings.

nodes.name: ServerConfigs.java
nodes.summary: Core broker configuration constants including broker.id, message.max.bytes, num.io.threads and their ConfigDef definitions.

nodes.name: BrokerHeartbeatManager.java
nodes.summary: Manages broker heartbeat soft state in the controller, tracking liveness, fencing status, and controlled shutdown for all registered brokers.

nodes.name: BrokerHeartbeatManager
nodes.summary: Tracks broker heartbeats, manages fencing/unfencing decisions, and determines which brokers should be shut down based on heartbeat timeouts.

nodes.name: ClientQuotaControlManager.java
nodes.summary: Controller-side manager for client quota configurations, handling quota alterations and producing quota metadata records.

nodes.name: ClientQuotaControlManager
nodes.summary: Manages client quota state in the controller, processing alter quota requests and replaying quota records.

nodes.name: ConfigurationControlManager.java
nodes.summary: Controller-side manager for dynamic configuration changes (broker, topic, client configs), handling incremental and legacy alter config operations with validation and policy enforcement.

nodes.name: ConfigurationControlManager
nodes.summary: Manages dynamic configuration state using timeline data structures, validates config changes against schema and policies, and produces config metadata records.

nodes.name: ControllerRequestContext.java
nodes.summary: Encapsulates context for controller requests including principal, deadline, and request header information for authorization and throttling.

nodes.name: PartitionChangeBuilder.java

nodes.name: PeriodicTaskControlManager.java

nodes.name: ProducerIdControlManager.java

nodes.name: RaftClientKRaftVersionAccessor.java

nodes.name: ReplicationControlManager.java

nodes.name: ReplicationControlManager

nodes.name: KRaftMetadataCache.java
nodes.summary: KRaft-based metadata cache that provides topic/partition metadata lookups from a MetadataImage, serving metadata requests for brokers in KRaft mode.

nodes.name: MetadataRecordSerde

nodes.name: MetadataVersionConfigValidator

nodes.name: TopicPartitionLog.java

nodes.name: RemoteLogManagerConfig

nodes.name: ProducerStateManagerConfig.java

nodes.name: ConsumerManager.java

nodes.summary: Interface for managing remote log segment metadata lifecycle: add, update, delete, and query operations.

nodes.name: RemoteLogMetadata.java

nodes.name: RemoteLogSegmentMetadata.java

nodes.summary: Configuration for the KRaft metadata log including segment size, retention, and snapshot intervals.

nodes.name: MetadataLogConfig
nodes.summary: Defines configuration properties for the Raft metadata log: segment bytes, retention, snapshot intervals, and log directory.

nodes.name: KafkaRaftClient.java
nodes.summary: Core Raft consensus protocol implementation for KRaft, handling leader election, log replication, voting, fetching, and quorum management.

nodes.name: KafkaRaftClient
nodes.summary: Implements the Raft protocol state machine: elections, vote/fetch/beginQuorumEpoch handling, log replication, snapshotting, and dynamic voter reconfiguration.

nodes.name: KafkaRaftClientDriver.java
nodes.summary: Thread driver for the KafkaRaftClient that processes requests and handles the event loop on a dedicated thread

nodes.name: KafkaRaftClientDriver
nodes.summary: ShutdownableThread that drives the KafkaRaftClient event loop, handling requests and completions

nodes.name: KRaftConfigs.java
nodes.summary: Configuration definitions for KRaft consensus protocol including election timeouts, fetch sizes, and controller quorum settings

nodes.name: KRaftConfigs
nodes.summary: Static configuration constants and ConfigDef definitions for KRaft protocol parameters

nodes.name: LogFetchInfo.java
nodes.summary: Simple data class holding fetch result information including records and log offset metadata

nodes.name: LogOffsetMetadata.java
nodes.summary: Metadata class representing a log offset along with associated epoch and segment position information

nodes.name: RaftClient.java
nodes.summary: Core interface for Raft client operations including leader election, log appending, snapshots, and listener registration

nodes.name: RaftClient

nodes.name: RaftLog.java
nodes.summary: Interface defining the Raft replicated log abstraction with methods for appending, reading, truncating, and snapshot management

nodes.name: RaftLog

nodes.name: RaftManager.java
nodes.summary: High-level interface for managing a Raft instance, providing access to the RaftClient, RaftLog, and request handling

nodes.name: RaftManager

nodes.name: RaftRequest.java
nodes.summary: Abstract base class for Raft protocol request messages with subclasses for inbound and outbound requests

nodes.name: RaftRequest

nodes.name: AbstractConfigurableStoreFactory.java

nodes.name: TaskTopicPartition.java
nodes.summary: Interface representing a topic partition assigned to a task in Kafka Streams task assignment

nodes.name: TopicPartitionMetadata.java
nodes.summary: Internal metadata class for topic partitions used in Streams processor internals

nodes.name: DefaultTaskTopicPartition.java
nodes.summary: Default implementation of TaskTopicPartition interface for Streams task assignment

nodes.name: StreamsBuilder.java
nodes.summary: High-level DSL entry point for building Kafka Streams topologies with stream, table, and globalTable sources

nodes.name: StreamsBuilder
nodes.summary: Primary DSL class for specifying Kafka Streams topologies, providing methods to create KStream, KTable, and GlobalKTable sources

nodes.name: StreamsConfig.java
nodes.summary: Comprehensive configuration class for Kafka Streams applications defining all streams-specific configs plus producer/consumer/admin overrides

nodes.name: StreamsConfig
nodes.summary: Central configuration class extending AbstractConfig with all Kafka Streams settings including threading, state, caching, and client configs

nodes.name: StreamsMetadata.java
nodes.summary: Interface representing metadata about a Kafka Streams instance including host info and assigned partitions

nodes.name: TaskMetadata.java
nodes.summary: Interface providing metadata about a Streams task including its ID, partitions, and committed offsets

nodes.name: TopologyConfig.java
nodes.summary: Configuration class for individual named topologies allowing per-topology overrides of Streams settings

nodes.name: TopologyConfig
nodes.summary: Per-topology configuration extending AbstractConfig, supporting overrides for deserialization handlers, caching, and store suppliers

nodes.name: InvalidStateStorePartitionException.java
nodes.summary: Exception thrown when accessing a state store with an invalid partition

nodes.name: LogAndContinueExceptionHandler.java
nodes.summary: Deserialization exception handler that logs the error and continues processing

nodes.name: LogAndContinueProcessingExceptionHandler.java
nodes.summary: Processing exception handler that logs the error and continues processing

nodes.name: LogAndFailExceptionHandler.java
nodes.summary: Deserialization exception handler that logs the error and fails the stream (default handler)

nodes.name: LogAndFailProcessingExceptionHandler.java
nodes.summary: Processing exception handler that logs the error and fails the stream (default handler)

nodes.name: StreamsUncaughtExceptionHandler.java
nodes.summary: Interface for handling uncaught exceptions in stream threads with options to replace thread, shutdown client, or shutdown application

nodes.name: StreamsConfigUtils.java
nodes.summary: Utility class with helper methods for extracting and processing StreamsConfig values

nodes.name: StreamsClientMetricsDelegatingReporter.java
nodes.summary: Metrics reporter that delegates Streams client metrics to a configurable reporter implementation

nodes.name: InternalStreamsBuilder.java
nodes.summary: Internal builder that constructs the Kafka Streams topology graph from high-level DSL operations (stream, table, globalTable). Handles topology optimization and graph node management.

nodes.name: InternalStreamsBuilder
nodes.summary: Core internal class that translates high-level KStream/KTable DSL calls into an internal topology graph of processor nodes, handling optimization passes.

nodes.name: MaterializedStoreFactory.java
nodes.summary: Abstract base class for store factories that wrap a MaterializedInternal instance to create state stores from Materialized configurations.

nodes.name: MaterializedStoreFactory
nodes.summary: Abstract store factory base class wrapping MaterializedInternal for creating state stores with configurable DSL store formats.

nodes.name: OuterStreamJoinStoreFactory.java
nodes.summary: Store factory for outer stream-stream joins, creating key-value stores that track unmatched join records with timestamps and join sides.

nodes.name: OuterStreamJoinStoreFactory
nodes.summary: StoreFactory implementation that creates stores for outer stream-stream joins, managing left/right unmatched records.

nodes.name: StreamsBuilder.scala
nodes.summary: Scala wrapper around the Java StreamsBuilder providing idiomatic Scala API with implicit serdes for building Kafka Streams topologies.

nodes.name: FunctionsCompatConversions.scala
nodes.summary: Implicit conversions from Scala functions to Java functional interfaces used by Kafka Streams (Mapper, Predicate, etc.).

nodes.name: ImplicitConversions.scala
nodes.summary: Implicit conversions between Scala KStream/KTable/KGroupedStream wrappers and their underlying Java counterparts.

nodes.name: package.scala
nodes.summary: Package object for Kafka Streams Scala module providing common type aliases and imports.

nodes.name: Branched.scala
nodes.summary: Scala wrapper for Branched class providing idiomatic Scala API for stream branching operations.

nodes.name: ConnectorClientConfigRequest.java
nodes.summary: Data class representing a request to override client configurations for a connector, including connector name, type, and client class.

nodes.name: ConnectorClientConfigOverridePolicy.java
nodes.summary: Interface defining the policy for which client configurations connectors are allowed to override.

nodes.name: Connector.java
nodes.summary: Abstract base class for Kafka Connect connectors that manage integration with external systems, responsible for task configuration generation and input monitoring.

nodes.name: Connector
nodes.summary: Abstract base class for all Kafka Connect connectors; manages lifecycle (start/stop), task config generation, and config validation.

nodes.name: ConnectorContext.java
nodes.summary: Interface providing connectors with runtime context to request task reconfiguration from the Connect framework.

nodes.name: ConnectRecord.java
nodes.summary: Abstract base record class for Kafka Connect holding topic, partition, key, value, schema, timestamp, and headers for source/sink records.

nodes.name: ConnectRecord
nodes.summary: Abstract base class for Connect records holding topic, partition, key/value with schemas, timestamp, and headers.

nodes.name: Task.java
nodes.summary: Interface for Kafka Connect tasks that perform the actual data transfer work, with start/stop lifecycle methods.

nodes.name: SchemaBuilder.java
nodes.summary: Fluent builder API for constructing Connect Schema objects including struct, array, map, and primitive types with optional metadata.

nodes.name: SchemaBuilder
nodes.summary: Fluent builder for creating Connect Schema instances with support for all types, fields, documentation, versioning, and defaults.

nodes.name: IllegalWorkerStateException.java
nodes.summary: Exception thrown when a Connect worker is in an illegal state.

nodes.name: AbstractConnectorClientConfigOverridePolicy.java
nodes.summary: Abstract base implementation of ConnectorClientConfigOverridePolicy providing common lifecycle methods.

nodes.name: AllConnectorClientConfigOverridePolicy.java
nodes.summary: Policy implementation that allows all client config overrides for connectors.

nodes.name: AllowlistConnectorClientConfigOverridePolicy.java
nodes.summary: Policy implementation allowing only allowlisted client config overrides for connectors.

nodes.name: NoneConnectorClientConfigOverridePolicy.java
nodes.summary: Policy implementation that disallows all client config overrides for connectors.

nodes.name: PrincipalConnectorClientConfigOverridePolicy.java
nodes.summary: Policy implementation allowing only principal-related (SASL) client config overrides for connectors.

nodes.name: AbstractWorkerSourceTask.java
nodes.summary: Abstract base class for worker source tasks handling record polling, conversion, producer sending, offset management, and metrics.

nodes.name: AbstractWorkerSourceTask
nodes.summary: Abstract base for source task execution in Connect workers; polls records from SourceTask, converts and sends to Kafka via Producer, manages offsets and metrics.

nodes.name: ConnectorConfig.java
nodes.summary: Configuration class defining all standard connector configuration properties including transforms, converters, error handling, and predicates.

nodes.summary: Defines and parses all standard connector configuration properties including name, class, tasks.max, transforms, converters, error tolerance, and predicates.

nodes.name: ExactlyOnceWorkerSourceTask.java
nodes.summary: Worker source task implementation providing exactly-once semantics using Kafka transactions for producing records and committing offsets atomically.

nodes.summary: Extends AbstractWorkerSourceTask to provide exactly-once delivery using Kafka producer transactions with atomic offset commits.

nodes.name: SinkConnectorConfig.java
nodes.summary: Configuration class for sink connectors extending ConnectorConfig with sink-specific properties like topics, topics.regex, and DLQ settings.

nodes.summary: Extends ConnectorConfig with sink-specific configuration including topics, topics.regex, and dead letter queue settings.

nodes.summary: Configuration class for source connectors, defining topic creation settings and exactly-once source support options.

nodes.name: SourceConnectorConfig
nodes.summary: Extends ConnectorConfig with source-specific settings including topic creation and transaction boundaries.

nodes.name: TaskConfig.java
nodes.summary: Simple configuration holder for Connect task class property.

nodes.name: TaskPluginsMetadata.java
nodes.summary: Metadata about plugins loaded for a Connect task, including converters and transformations.

nodes.name: TopicCreationConfig.java
nodes.summary: Configuration definitions for automatic topic creation in source connectors including partitions, replication, and topic matching.

nodes.name: WorkerConfig.java
nodes.summary: Base configuration for Kafka Connect workers including bootstrap servers, plugin paths, and internal topic settings.

nodes.name: WorkerConfig
nodes.summary: AbstractConfig subclass defining all configuration properties for Connect worker processes.

nodes.name: WorkerConfigTransformer.java
nodes.summary: Transforms connector configurations by resolving variable references through config providers.

nodes.name: SetSchemaMetadata.java
nodes.summary: SMT (Single Message Transform) that updates schema name and version on Connect records.

nodes.summary: Predicate that evaluates to true when a record has a null value (tombstone).

nodes.name: TopicNameMatches.java

nodes.name: GroupCoordinatorService.java
nodes.summary: Top-level service implementing the GroupCoordinator interface, routing group operations to partition-level coordinators.

nodes.name: GroupCoordinatorService
nodes.summary: Implements GroupCoordinator interface handling heartbeats, joins, syncs, offset commits and consumer group lifecycle across partitions.

nodes.name: GroupMetadataManager.java
nodes.summary: Core state machine managing consumer group, classic group, and share group metadata including member joins, rebalances, and partition assignment.

nodes.name: GroupMetadataManager
nodes.summary: Manages all group types (consumer, classic, share) handling heartbeats, rebalance protocols, assignment, and group state transitions.

nodes.name: KafkaProducer.java
nodes.summary: Main Kafka producer client implementation supporting async sends, transactions, partitioning, and serialization.

nodes.name: KafkaProducer
nodes.summary: Thread-safe producer client that batches records, manages metadata, and supports idempotent/transactional production.

nodes.name: Producer.java
nodes.summary: Interface defining the Kafka producer API including send, flush, transactions, and metrics.

nodes.name: Producer
nodes.summary: Interface for Kafka producer operations: send, flush, initTransactions, beginTransaction, commitTransaction, abortTransaction.

nodes.name: ProducerConfig.java
nodes.summary: Configuration class for Kafka producer defining all producer properties like batch size, linger, acks, retries.

nodes.summary: Data class representing a record to be sent to Kafka with topic, partition, key, value, timestamp, and headers.

nodes.name: KafkaConsumer.java

nodes.summary: Consumer client that delegates to internal ConsumerDelegate, supporting both classic and new consumer group protocols.

nodes.name: Consumer.java
nodes.summary: Interface defining the Kafka consumer API including subscribe, poll, commit, seek, and partition assignment.

nodes.name: Consumer
nodes.summary: Interface for Kafka consumer operations: subscribe, poll, commit, seek, pause, resume, and offset management.

nodes.name: ConsumerConfig.java
nodes.summary: Configuration class for Kafka consumer defining properties like group.id, auto.offset.reset, max.poll.records.

nodes.summary: Data class representing a consumed record with topic, partition, offset, timestamp, key, value, and headers.

nodes.name: Admin.java
nodes.summary: Interface defining the Kafka admin client API for managing topics, ACLs, configs, consumer groups, and cluster operations.

nodes.summary: Interface for administrative Kafka operations: create/delete topics, alter configs, manage ACLs, describe cluster.

nodes.name: KafkaAdminClient.java
nodes.summary: Concrete implementation of Admin interface handling all administrative operations via network requests to brokers.

nodes.summary: Core network layer client managing connections, request sending/receiving, and protocol versioning for all Kafka clients.

nodes.summary: Represents a topic partition on a broker. Manages leader/follower state, ISR, log operations, high watermark advancement, and replica fetching.

nodes.name: ReplicaManager.scala
nodes.summary: Manages all partition replicas on a broker. Handles produce/fetch requests, replica fetching, leader/follower transitions, and ISR changes.

nodes.name: KafkaConfig.scala
nodes.summary: Kafka broker configuration class. Wraps and exposes all broker config properties including networking, storage, replication, and security settings.

nodes.name: KafkaConfig

nodes.name: KafkaApis.scala
nodes.summary: Request handler that dispatches all Kafka API requests to appropriate handlers. The central routing layer for all client and inter-broker RPCs.

nodes.name: KafkaApis

nodes.name: QuorumController.java
nodes.summary: The active KRaft controller that manages cluster metadata. Handles broker registrations, topic CRUD, partition reassignment, and configuration changes via Raft-replicated log.

nodes.name: QuorumController

nodes.name: ClusterControlManager.java
nodes.summary: Manages broker registrations and fencing within the KRaft controller. Tracks broker lifecycle including registration, heartbeats, and controlled shutdown.

nodes.name: MetadataImage.java

nodes.summary: Represents the state of a KRaft follower node. Tracks the current leader, fetch timeouts, and snapshot transfer progress.

nodes.name: QuorumState.java
nodes.summary: Manages KRaft quorum state transitions between Unattached, Prospective, Candidate, Voted, Leader, Follower, and Resigned states.

nodes.name: LogSegment.java

nodes.name: LogConfig.java
nodes.summary: Topic-level log configuration. Defines and validates topic config properties like retention, segment size, compression, and cleanup policy.

nodes.name: KafkaStreams.java
nodes.summary: Main entry point for Kafka Streams applications. Manages stream processing lifecycle including starting, stopping, state transitions, and interactive queries.

nodes.name: KafkaStreams
nodes.summary: Primary client class for running a Kafka Streams application, managing thread lifecycle, state stores, and metrics.

nodes.name: Topology.java
nodes.summary: Defines the processing topology as a DAG of sources, processors, and sinks. Used to build the stream processing graph before starting KafkaStreams.

nodes.name: Topology
nodes.summary: Builder-style class for constructing a stream processing DAG of source, processor, and sink nodes.

nodes.name: Processor.java
nodes.summary: Core functional interface for stream processors. Defines init, process, and close lifecycle methods for record processing.

nodes.name: Processor
nodes.summary: Functional interface for processing key-value records in a Kafka Streams topology with typed input/output.

nodes.name: KStream.java
nodes.summary: Abstraction of a record stream providing DSL operations like filter, map, flatMap, join, groupBy, and writing to topics.

nodes.name: KStream
nodes.summary: DSL interface representing an unbounded record stream with transformation operators (map, filter, join, aggregate, etc.).

nodes.name: KTable.java
nodes.summary: Abstraction of a changelog stream / materialized table. Provides DSL operations like filter, mapValues, join, groupBy, and state store queries.

nodes.name: KTable
nodes.summary: DSL interface representing a changelog-backed table with transformation and join operators.

nodes.name: KeyValueStore.java
nodes.summary: Interface for a key-value state store supporting put, get, delete, and range queries in Kafka Streams.

nodes.name: KeyValueStore

nodes.name: StreamThread.java
nodes.summary: Main processing thread for Kafka Streams that polls records from consumers, manages task execution, handles rebalancing, and coordinates state restoration. Implements the core event loop for stream processing.

nodes.name: StreamThread
nodes.summary: Core processing thread that runs the main poll-process loop for Kafka Streams applications, coordinating consumer polling, task execution, commits, and partition rebalancing.

nodes.name: TaskManager.java
nodes.summary: Manages the lifecycle of stream processing tasks including creation, assignment, revocation, committing offsets, and closing tasks. Coordinates active and standby tasks.

nodes.name: TaskManager
nodes.summary: Manages stream task lifecycle including creation, assignment, revocation, offset commits, and cleanup. Handles both active and standby tasks with support for exactly-once semantics.

nodes.name: SourceConnector.java
nodes.summary: Abstract base class for Connect source connectors that pull data from external systems into Kafka. Provides exactly-once support signaling and transaction boundary configuration.

nodes.name: SourceConnector
nodes.summary: Abstract base class for source connectors that import data from external systems into Kafka topics, with support for exactly-once semantics and transaction boundaries.

nodes.name: SinkConnector.java
nodes.summary: Abstract base class for Connect sink connectors that consume data from Kafka topics and write to external systems. Supports offset alteration/reset.

nodes.name: SinkConnector
nodes.summary: Abstract base class for sink connectors that export data from Kafka topics to external systems.

nodes.name: SourceTask.java
nodes.summary: Abstract base class for Connect source tasks that poll records from external systems. Defines transaction boundary modes and commit semantics.

nodes.name: SourceTask
nodes.summary: Abstract task that polls records from an external system for delivery to Kafka, with configurable transaction boundaries (poll, interval, connector-defined).

nodes.name: SinkTask.java
nodes.summary: Abstract base class for Connect sink tasks that receive records from Kafka and write them to external systems. Defines put/flush/preCommit lifecycle.

nodes.name: SinkTask
nodes.summary: Abstract task that receives records from Kafka and writes them to an external system, with partition assignment lifecycle (open/put/flush/close).

nodes.name: Worker.java
nodes.summary: Core Connect runtime component that manages connector and task instances on a single node. Handles starting/stopping connectors and tasks, configuring producers/consumers, and managing offsets.

nodes.name: Worker
nodes.summary: Manages connector and task instances on a Connect worker node, handling their full lifecycle including configuration, creation, execution, and shutdown.

nodes.name: DistributedHerder.java
nodes.summary: Distributed mode implementation of the Herder interface that coordinates connectors and tasks across a cluster of Connect workers using a Kafka-based group protocol for leader election and work assignment.

nodes.name: DistributedHerder
nodes.summary: Implements distributed connector/task coordination across a Connect cluster using Kafka group protocol for rebalancing, configuration storage, and REST API forwarding.

nodes.name: ConnectMetrics.java
nodes.summary: Metrics infrastructure for Kafka Connect that wraps the Kafka Metrics library, providing metric groups, sensors, and JMX reporting for connectors and tasks.

nodes.name: ConnectMetrics
nodes.summary: Provides configurable metrics infrastructure for Connect workers with support for metric groups, sensors, gauges, and pluggable reporters.

layers.name: Client Libraries
layers.description: Producer, consumer, and admin client implementations for interacting with Kafka brokers

layers.name: Protocol & Common
layers.description: Shared protocol definitions, serialization, security, and common data types used across all Kafka components

layers.name: Broker Core
layers.description: Core broker server logic including request handling, replication, networking, and server lifecycle

layers.name: KRaft Consensus
layers.description: Raft consensus protocol implementation for metadata replication and leader election

layers.name: Metadata Management
layers.description: KRaft controller logic and metadata image management for cluster state including topics, partitions, brokers, and configurations

layers.name: Storage Engine
layers.description: Log storage, segment management, and remote/tiered storage for Kafka data

layers.name: Kafka Streams
layers.description: Stream processing library with DSL and Processor API for building real-time data processing applications

layers.name: Kafka Connect
layers.description: Framework for building and running connectors that stream data between Kafka and external systems

layers.name: Coordinators
layers.description: Group, share, and transaction coordinator implementations managing consumer groups and transactional state

layers.name: Configuration
layers.description: Runtime configuration files for brokers, controllers, consumers, producers, and Connect workers

layers.name: Build & Infrastructure
layers.description: Build system, CI/CD pipelines, Docker tooling, shell scripts, and project documentation

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: Start with the README and entry point to understand Kafka as a distributed event streaming platform. The shell script launches the broker via the core Kafka.scala main class.

tour.title: Build System
tour.description: Kafka uses Gradle with a multi-module structure. The root build.gradle defines shared settings, settings.gradle lists all subprojects, and dependencies.gradle centralizes version management.

tour.title: Client Libraries — Producer
tour.description: The Producer API lets applications publish records to Kafka topics. KafkaProducer is the main implementation with async sends, batching, partitioning, and transaction support.

tour.title: Client Libraries — Consumer & Admin
tour.description: The Consumer API polls records from topics with group-based coordination. The Admin API manages topics, ACLs, and cluster operations. NetworkClient is the shared network layer underneath all clients.

tour.title: Broker Server Architecture
tour.description: The broker handles client requests via KafkaApis (the central request router), manages partitions through ReplicaManager, and uses a NIO SocketServer for networking. BrokerServer orchestrates the full lifecycle in KRaft mode.

tour.title: KRaft Consensus Layer
tour.description: KRaft replaces ZooKeeper for metadata consensus. KafkaRaftClient implements the Raft protocol with leader election, log replication, and quorum state management. QuorumState handles transitions between leader, follower, candidate, and other states.

tour.title: Metadata Management & Controller
tour.description: The QuorumController is the active controller managing all cluster metadata. It uses ReplicationControlManager for topics/partitions, ClusterControlManager for broker registrations, and MetadataImage as an immutable snapshot of cluster state.

tour.title: Storage Engine
tour.description: Kafka's storage is based on append-only log segments. LogSegment handles on-disk I/O, LogConfig defines topic-level storage settings, and the Remote Storage API enables tiered storage for offloading old segments to external systems.

tour.title: Kafka Streams
tour.description: Kafka Streams is a client library for building stream processing applications. KafkaStreams is the entry point, StreamsBuilder provides the high-level DSL, and StreamThread runs the core processing loop with task management.

tour.title: Kafka Connect
tour.description: Kafka Connect is a framework for streaming data between Kafka and external systems. Connectors (Source/Sink) define the integration, Tasks do the actual work, Workers execute them, and DistributedHerder coordinates across a cluster.

tour.title: Infrastructure — Docker & CI/CD
tour.description: Kafka provides Docker image build/test/release tooling and GitHub Actions CI pipelines for automated testing across JDK versions, Docker image validation, and release promotion.
